name: Tunda
description: 2HP cascaded buffer system with four normalled stages plus an averaged sum out. One module covers buffered mult, precision mixer, 2:2 split, attenuator and amplifier duties for audio and CV — linkable with more Tundas for longer chains.

inputs.name: In 1
inputs.description: First stage input. Head of the cascade — whatever goes here shows up on Out 1 and, via normalling, is summed into every stage below it unless you break the chain by plugging into a later input.

inputs.name: In 2
inputs.description: Second stage input. Normalled to the first stage so Out 2 carries In 1 + In 2. Plugging here breaks the normal from In 1 for stages 2–4.

inputs.name: In 3
inputs.description: Third stage input. Normalled from above; patching here breaks the chain at stage 3.

inputs.name: In 4
inputs.description: Fourth stage input. Normalled from above. Patching here breaks the chain at the last stage.

outputs.name: Out 1
outputs.description: Buffered tap after stage 1. Use it as a clean copy of In 1 or as the head of a buffered mult chain.

outputs.name: Out 2
outputs.description: Buffered tap after stage 2. If In 2 is empty this mirrors Out 1; otherwise it carries the running sum of stages 1–2.

outputs.name: Out 3
outputs.description: Buffered tap after stage 3. Either a mult copy of the upstream signal or a running sum of stages 1–3, depending on what's patched.

outputs.name: Out 4
outputs.description: Buffered tap after stage 4. With all four inputs patched this is the full 4:1 sum (precision x2/x3/x4 amp when fewer duplicates are used).

outputs.name: Average Out
outputs.description: Averaged sum of the four stages — divides the combined level back down so a 4:1 mix stays inside ±10V without clipping. Great for CV averaging and safe voice summing.

behaviors.name: Cascade normalling
behaviors.trigger: patch into a later input to break the chain
behaviors.description: The four stages are normalled together top-to-bottom. Plugging into In 2/3/4 breaks the normal at that point, which is what lets a single module reshape between mult, mix and split roles without a switch.

behaviors.name: 1:4 buffered mult
behaviors.trigger: one source into In 1, leave In 2/3/4 empty
behaviors.description: Out 1–4 all carry a buffered copy of In 1. No sag on V/Oct, no loading when driving many destinations.

behaviors.name: 4:1 precision sum
behaviors.trigger: four sources into In 1–4, take Out 4
behaviors.description: Out 4 is the running sum of all four stages. Useful when you want the full combined level; use Average Out instead if you need to stay inside ±10V.

behaviors.name: 2:2 split
behaviors.trigger: source A into In 1, source B into In 3
behaviors.description: Out 1–2 carry source A (B is blocked at stage 3's break), Out 3–4 carry source B. Two independent buffered pairs in 2HP.

behaviors.name: Precision gain x2 / x3 / x4
behaviors.trigger: duplicate one source across consecutive inputs and take the last used Out
behaviors.description: Copy In 1 into In 2 (and 3, 4) to get 2x, 3x or 4x gain at Out 2/3/4 — handy for scaling a 1V/oct bus to higher octaves or boosting quiet CV.

behaviors.name: Precision attenuation 0.75 / 0.5 / 0.25
behaviors.trigger: feed one source, take Average Out with 1–4 stages engaged
behaviors.description: Average Out divides by 4. Combine with empty stages or identical sources to land on 0.25x, 0.5x, 0.75x or 1x scaling — clean, calibrated CV attenuation with no pots to drift.

behaviors.name: Chainable
behaviors.trigger: patch Out 4 of one Tunda into In 1 of another
behaviors.description: Cascade keeps going across modules — two Tundas give you an 8-stage buffered chain, three give you 12, etc.

patchIdeas.name: Buffered V/Oct fan-out to four VCOs
patchIdeas.patch: One sequencer CV drives four oscillators in tune via the cascade normalling.
steps: Patch Sequencer (Pitch CV) into Tunda IN 1.
Leave IN 2, IN 3 and IN 4 empty — the normal copies IN 1 down the chain.
Patch Tunda OUT 1 into VCO 1 V/Oct.
Patch Tunda OUT 2 into VCO 2 V/Oct.
Patch Tunda OUT 3 into VCO 3 V/Oct.
Patch Tunda OUT 4 into VCO 4 V/Oct.
Detune each VCO by ear — the pitch CV itself stays identical across all four.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Tunda OUT 1–4 — four buffered copies of the same V/Oct, no tuning droop.
patchIdeas.hear: Four oscillators tracking in perfect unison across the keyboard. Detune by cents at each VCO for a thick supersaw/chorus stack without pitch drift as you add more voices.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Tunda:
Sequencer:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Tunda (In 1)
- Tunda (Out 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Tunda (Out 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Tunda (Out 3) p> VCO 3 (V/Oct)
- Tunda (Out 4) p> VCO 4 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Safe 4-voice summing mixer
patchIdeas.patch: Average Out sums four voices with built-in divide-by-4 so the bus stays inside ±10V.
steps: Patch Voice 1 OUT into Tunda IN 1.
Patch Voice 2 OUT into Tunda IN 2.
Patch Voice 3 OUT into Tunda IN 3.
Patch Voice 4 OUT into Tunda IN 4.
Patch Tunda AVERAGE OUT into your main output / recorder input.
If a voice sounds too quiet in the mix, boost it at its source — Tunda keeps the gain structure exact, it doesn't have per-channel pots.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Tunda AVERAGE OUT — the four voices combined at 0.25x each, staying clipless.
patchIdeas.hear: A clean, balanced four-voice sum. Level stays controlled no matter how loud the inputs are — trade per-channel volume control for guaranteed headroom.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Tunda:
Voice 1:
Voice 2:
Voice 3:
Voice 4:
Output:

- Voice 1 (Out) -> Tunda (In 1)
- Voice 2 (Out) -> Tunda (In 2)
- Voice 3 (Out) -> Tunda (In 3)
- Voice 4 (Out) -> Tunda (In 4)
- Tunda (Average Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Precision CV scaler (0.5x attenuator)
patchIdeas.patch: Cut any CV in half cleanly using Average Out with two copies of the same signal.
steps: Patch your source CV (e.g. an LFO) into Tunda IN 1.
Mult the same source and patch it into Tunda IN 2 as well.
Leave IN 3 and IN 4 empty.
Patch Tunda AVERAGE OUT into the destination (e.g. filter cutoff).
The destination now sees exactly 0.5x the source — add a third copy into IN 3 for 0.75x, or use one copy alone for 0.25x.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Tunda AVERAGE OUT — calibrated fraction of the source (0.25 / 0.5 / 0.75 / 1.0).
patchIdeas.hear: The modulation destination moves by a precise, repeatable fraction of the source. No pot wobble — useful for dialing in exact transposition amounts or matching two modulation depths.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Tunda:
LFO:
Filter:

- LFO (Out) -> Tunda (In 1)
- LFO (Out) -> Tunda (In 2) // duplicate for 0.5x
- Tunda (Average Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: 2:2 split — two independent buffered branches
patchIdeas.patch: One Tunda acts as two separate stereo-style buffered mults in 2HP.
steps: Patch Source A (e.g. a pitch CV) into Tunda IN 1 — leave IN 2 empty so the normal carries A into stage 2.
Patch Source B (e.g. an LFO) into Tunda IN 3 — this breaks the chain at stage 3.
Take Tunda OUT 1 and OUT 2 as two buffered copies of Source A.
Take Tunda OUT 3 and OUT 4 as two buffered copies of Source B.
AVERAGE OUT is still live — it will output the average of A, A, B, B (= 0.5·A + 0.5·B) if you want a bonus combined signal.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1–2 = Source A (x2 copies). OUT 3–4 = Source B (x2 copies).
patchIdeas.hear: Two completely independent buffered pairs. Great for sending one pitch CV to two oscillators while simultaneously sending one LFO to two filters — no crosstalk between the pairs.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Tunda:
Pitch CV:
LFO:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:
Filter 1:
Filter 2:

- Pitch CV (Out) p> Tunda (In 1)
- LFO (Out) >> Tunda (In 3) // breaks the chain
- Tunda (Out 1) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Tunda (Out 2) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
- Tunda (Out 3) >> Filter 1 (Cutoff)
- Tunda (Out 4) >> Filter 2 (Cutoff)
